element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learn movement, stamina management, cursor-based interaction
Understand spatial layout, item collection, door/object interaction

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After Mary leaves to find Mr. Barrows

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Panic mode mechanics, hiding spots, evasion over combat
Find safe zones, use environment strategically to survive
Balance running vs. walking, use rest recovery mechanic
Collect items for solving environment puzzles and accessing areas

element_introduction_order.element: Story branch choices (Simon vs. father corpse discovery)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Determine narrative path and ending type

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Late game after collecting specific items/clues
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Use Devil Idol or Scepter, navigate catacombs, approach final sequences

element_introduction_order.element: Mary Barrows revelation and final confrontation
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culmination of narrative threads, multiple ending possibilities

typical_relief_triggers: Sitting on floor to recover stamina
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Asymmetric tension cycle: safe exploration punctuated by sudden lethal threat encounters. Stamina system creates secondary micro-cycles of exertion and recovery. Multiple branching paths extend exploration phases, allowing player control over pacing rhythm.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side rooms, courtyard areas, and inaccessible zones contain optional story clues (Simon jail cell, father's corpse, pool area, library secrets). Player agency determines which narrative branches are discovered.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden rooms revealed through clue collection and inventory use (catacombs accessed via Devil Idol or Scepter). Occult church leads to masked basement areas. Missing annotations suggest cut content: disused elevator, unreachable library hole-in-wall trap, inaccessible courtyard pool, west wing broken door.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe exploration teaches movement, stamina, and interaction mechanics before threat introduction. Player acclimation period.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Scissorman encounters force panic mode management. Item collection and hiding spot discovery become survival skills. Branching exploration increases complexity as story splits between Simon and father paths.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Catacombs section escalates spatial complexity. Final confrontation with Mary Barrows and resolution selection based on accumulated choices and item collection.
notable_spikes: First Scissorman encounter (sudden threat paradigm shift)
Health depletion during extended chase sequences (panic mode pressure)
Catacombs navigation without clear safe zones

notable_level_patterns.description: Mansion interior rooms function as safe exploration spaces where player learns layout, collects items, and discovers clues without immediate threat. Stamina regeneration possible here.
Upon Scissorman spawn, safe areas become dangerous. Same spaces now require evasion and hiding. Spatial familiarity becomes tactical advantage.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Foyer and corridors transition from exploration to danger zones

notable_level_patterns.description: Late-game areas (catacombs, church) locked behind item collection and story progression. Branching paths create divergent spatial experiences based on player choices.

Inaccessible exits and broken mechanics suggest cut content: elevator non-functional, pool area unreachable, library traps disabled, west wing door broken. Spatial design hints at larger original scope.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Courtyard pool, elevator area, library wall hole, west wing

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Clock Tower SNES article)
